back_translation: The LORD said to Joshua, 'Do not be afraid or discouraged. Take with you the whole army, and rise up, go up to Ai. See, I have given into your hand the king of Ai, his people, his city, and his land.
You shall do to Ai and its king as you did to Jericho and its king, except that you may take its plunder and livestock for yourselves. Set an ambush behind the city.'
So Joshua and all the army rose up to go up to Ai. Joshua chose 30,000 valiant warriors and sent them out at night,
and he commanded them: 'Look — you shall lie in ambush behind the city. Do not go very far from the city. All of you be ready.
I and all the troops with me will approach the city. When they come out to meet us as the first time, we will flee before them.
They will pursue us until we have drawn them away from the city, for they will say, "They are fleeing before us as the first time." So we will flee before them.
Then you shall rise from the ambush and take the city, and the LORD your God will give it into your hand.
And when you have taken the city, set the city on fire. Do according to the word of the LORD. See, I have given you orders.'
So Joshua sent them, and they went to the place of ambush and stayed between Bethel and Ai, west of Ai. But Joshua spent that night among the troops.
Joshua got up early in the morning and mustered the troops, and he and the elders of Israel went up before the troops to Ai.
All the troops of war who were with him went up, approached, and came in front of the city. They camped on the north side of Ai, with the valley between them and Ai.
Joshua had taken about five thousand men and set them as an ambush between Bethel and Ai, west of the city.
So they stationed the troops — all the camp that was north of the city, and the rear guard west of the city. And Joshua went that night into the valley.

When the king of Ai saw them, the men of the city hurried and rose early and went out to meet Israel for battle — he and all his people — at the appointed place in front of the Arabah. But he did not know that there was an ambush against him behind the city.
Joshua and all Israel pretended to be beaten before them and fled by way of the wilderness.
All the people who were in Ai were called together to pursue them. They pursued Joshua and were drawn away from the city.
Not a man was left in Ai or Bethel who did not go out after Israel. They left the city open and pursued Israel.
The LORD said to Joshua, 'Stretch out the battle lance in your hand toward Ai, for I will give it into your hand.' So Joshua stretched out the battle lance that was in his hand toward the city.
And the ambush rose quickly from its place. They ran as he stretched out his hand, entered the city, captured it, and quickly set the city on fire.

When the men of Ai turned around behind them, they saw — behold, the smoke of the city was rising to the sky. They had no power to flee this way or that way. And the people who had fled to the wilderness turned against the pursuers.
Joshua and all Israel saw that the ambush had captured the city and that the smoke of the city was rising. They turned around and struck down the men of Ai.
Meanwhile, those came out from the city to meet them, and they were to Israel in the middle — these from this side and these from that side. They struck them down until none survived or escaped.
But the king of Ai they captured alive and brought him to Joshua.

When Israel had finished killing all the inhabitants of Ai in the field, in the wilderness where they had pursued them, and all of them had fallen by the edge of the sword until they were finished, all Israel returned to Ai and struck it down by the edge of the sword.
Those who fell that day, from man to woman, were twelve thousand — all the people of Ai.
Joshua did not draw back his hand which he had stretched out with the lance until he had devoted all the inhabitants of Ai to destruction.
Only the livestock and the plunder of that city Israel took as plunder for themselves, according to the word of the LORD which He had commanded Joshua.
So Joshua burned Ai and made it a perpetual heap of ruins, a desolation, to this day.

He hanged the king of Ai on a tree until the time of evening; and at sunset Joshua commanded, and they took his corpse down from the tree and threw it at the entrance of the city gate. And they raised over him a large heap of stones — to this day.
Then Joshua built an altar to the LORD, the God of Israel, on Mount Ebal,

just as Moses the servant of the LORD had commanded the people of Israel, as it is written in the Book of the Law of Moses — an altar of uncut stones on which no iron tool had been used. And on it they offered burnt offerings to the LORD, and they sacrificed peace offerings.
And there on the stones he wrote a copy of the law of Moses, which he had written before the people of Israel.

All Israel — foreigners and natives alike — with their elders, officers, and judges, stood on this side and on that side of the ark before the Levitical priests who carried the ark of the covenant of the LORD. Half of them faced Mount Gerizim, and half of them faced Mount Ebal, as Moses the servant of the LORD had commanded earlier, to bless the people of Israel.
After that, Joshua read all the words of the law — the blessing and the curse — according to all that is written in the Book of the Law.
There was not a word from all that Moses had commanded that Joshua did not read before the entire assembly of Israel, including the women, the little ones, and the foreigners who walked among them.
